kadePhillips.subheaderLeft: "You Must Take Care Of Yourself First"
body: Kade is big-hearted, deeply caring toward family and friends, and socially magnetic despite being in a profession many frame as "less than manly." He reminds you of the sort of man you wanted your sons to become.
There is also a darker edge: he carries deep anger, and he spends time with people who stoke it.

ladyAvelineDubois.subheaderLeft: Seventh Generation Ancilla of Clan Malkavian
body: Lady Aveline Dubois found you through the occult, through omens, and through the lonely hunger of a woman already half in love with mystery. She gave you the Blood, but also wonder: divination, prophecy, madness, freedom, and the terrible comfort of being understood. Then, as prophets and wanderers often do, she vanished, leaving you with riddles where certainty should have been.

thePieceTaker.subheaderLeft: Seventh Generation Elder of the Banu Haqim
body: Laleh bint Saif did not leave behind easy answers. She was the mystery at the center of your Blood, the missing piece of your lineage, and eventually the terror Toronto knew as the Piece-Taker. When you found her, she was no courtly sire waiting with comfort, but an Autarkis wrapped in solitude, violence, and old purpose. She sheltered you, taught you, and taught you the harsh lesson that lineage does not always bring peace.

Enzo is a retired Toronto Mafia hitman with a blissfully ignorant family. Now retired on blood money, he spends his time on charity and feeding stray dogs. He still keeps a hidden phone, though he has nearly forgotten about it; the Don still has the number. You have followed Enzo since childhood in the form of an arctic wolf, which Enzo believes to be a succession of huskies.

drake.subheaderLeft: Seventh Generation Elder of Clan Nosferatu
body: Drake chose you because you endured. Before the Embrace, you were useful: overlooked, clever, hard to kill, and capable of hearing what powerful men thought no one heard. Afterward, you became something more deliberate: Drake's instrument, student, and eventual successor beneath Toronto. His public disappearance left you holding a throne of tunnels, secrets, rats, old paths, and older obligations.

mara.subheaderLeft: Ninth Generation Ancilla of Clan Nosferatu
body: Oksana Ziva ("Mara") is your blood made spectacle. She took the savagery in your lineage and sharpened it into doctrine, joining the Sabbat and hunting the powerful with theatrical cruelty. Every murder she commits is both execution and sermon: the powerful are prey, walls do not save them, and fear is more useful when it is witnessed. She is your childe, your shame, your warning, and one of the few monsters in Toronto who might understand exactly what you are.

palis.subheaderLeft: Ninth Generation Neonate of Clan Nosferatu
body: Yusuf bin Yafa ("Palis") is proof that Clan Nosferatu does not only survive in tunnels. He built his mask out of screens, avatars, streams, arguments, and the endless noise of the internet. Where older Nosferatu gather secrets from rats and storm drains, Palis gathers them from chat logs, flame wars, parasocial attachments, and people who forget the unseen are still listening. He is irritating, useful, modern, and very much yours.

Miriam is a passionate and animated curator at the Royal Ontario Museum, also teaching at the University of Toronto. Clumsy outside archives, she secretly writes raunchy historical fiction, and rumors say she holds a fragment of mystical Kindred lore.

oliverGagnon.subheaderLeft: Seventh Generation Ancilla of Clan Toreador
body: Oliver Gagnon did not merely Embrace you. He curated you. Under his eye, your earliest nights became a gilded education in Toreador refinement, Camarilla etiquette, lineage, salons, beauty, and power. He taught you to understand immortality as performance, status as architecture, and culture as a weapon best wielded with a smile.

damienAbanda.subheaderLeft: Ninth Generation Ancilla of Clan Toreador
body: Damien Abanda was your attempt to pass the flame onward, and perhaps your first great failure as a sire. Embraced before your flight westward, he survived your tutelage, your indulgences, and your disappearance. Tonight he stands among the Anarchs as a polished diplomat of Red Flag, close enough to your court to wound you and distant enough to deny your authority.

keiraVaughan.subheaderLeft: "My Soldiers Must Be Able to Handle Anything"
body: Keira Vaughan is Toronto's police chief. She briefly caught a Masquerade breach before it was scrubbed, tied to one of Mara's dramatic murders of a political official. She is now investigating what she believes is a conspiracy of criminals capable of impossible feats of strength and speed. You admire her ability to bring lieutenants into the fold and convince them fully; her stern leadership reminds you of your days as a ship captain.

jervaisDeLaCroix.subheaderLeft: Seventh Generation Elder of Clan Tremere
body: Jervais bani Tremere made you with purpose, not love. He was an old architect of hidden loyalties, a Tremere whose allegiance ran through Goratrix and into Sabbat conspiracy. He Embraced you to use you: a captain, a survivor, a weapon planted in the New World. You escaped him, or killed him, or convinced yourself the difference doesn't matter. Either way, his shadow remains, casting its pall whenever you are reminded of your treachery—and the consequences of its discovery.

severinKoslov.subheaderLeft: Ninth Generation Neonate of Clan Tremere
body: Severin Koslov was chosen because he would not look away. As a mortal, he found irregularities where others saw paperwork, danger where others saw inconvenience, and he kept pulling the thread. You rewarded that stubbornness with the Blood. Now he is still under Accounting, still unfinished, and already entangled with the Hecata's lessons in death. He is a soldier being made, but not entirely by your hand.
29-year-old 'made man' stuck cooking the books and serving as go-between for you and Bacchus. He endures it, hoping to be Embraced while still young.

evanescentBlue.subheaderLeft: Ninth Generation Ancilla of Clan Ravnos
body: Evanescent Blue is a Ravnos survivor you saved near Kingston, now an Anarch source of diaspora news, warnings, and perspective.

merrickSingh.subheaderLeft: Eleventh Generation Neonate of Clan Ravnos
body: A Ravnos survivor you saved near Kingston, Merrick now masquerades as a Toreador under the supervision of your sire, Oliver Gagnon.
He blends charm, musical oddity, and misdirection—easy to overlook, and therefore easily underestimated and dangerous in a city that values secrets.

A ghoul from the Giovanni gifted to Caesar that was killed in an internal spat during the early 1900s. Her mere presence makes the Hecata uncomfortable due to her hatred of her former family. Most intimidating wraith of the trio. Sees her relationship with Caesar as transactional and a path towards revenge.

theBleakBokor.subheaderLeft: Tenth Generation Ancilla of the Samedi
body: You and the Bleak Bokor shaped Kindred society in the Caribbean together, especially during the Haitian Revolution. You were both founders of House High Saturday, and though he's no longer a member, your connection endures.
An ancient sorcerer wraith who bonded to you from Ceoris. He urges you to rebuild High Saturday and looks to you as a new hope for the Clan.

The closest wraith in your retinue, you have known each other for 3 centuries. Very level-headed and tactically minded but also becomes overly worried about 'his captain' easily. Can take drastic measures in an attempt at protection.

Jean and his family are the culmination of 100 years of your work to make your own personal mystic soldiers. The patriarch himself is mild-mannered and charming with the air of an academic, and is much older than he looks.

Despite hailing from the Ducheski stronghold in Poland, Jezdimir has little knowledge of his family's purpose. He looks and acts like an Eastern European hooligan, his petty criminal background is a surprising asset in redwork.

22 year-old journalism student at the University of Toronto. You befriended her mother, Camille Bouchard, shortly before Camille divorced her husband. Marie blames you for her parent's divorce (she's right).

Descendant of Lord Cavendish who, in 1845, leapt on your former family in Ireland the moment the potato blight made their estates vulnerable to financial takeover. You have been actively and with extreme pettiness fucking with his life. If Percival ever confronts you, only then will he learn why his life has been ruined... and then he will die.

A hacker/internet security professional who is legitimately employed, genuinely professional. Legitimate employment gives her access to limited spytech and surveillance equipment. She has monitoring tripwires set in various parts of the city, to warn of suspicious electronic activity. She is a massive fan of I.Q.'s VTube channel.
